title: How Marconi Gave Us the Wireless World
subtitle: 百年前的预言家：马可尼如何想象无线世界

paragraphs.en: A hundred years before iconic figures like Bill Gates and Steve Jobs permeated our lives, an Irish-Italian inventor laid the foundation of the communication explosion of the 21st century. Guglielmo Marconi was arguably the first truly global figure in modern communication. Not only was he the first to communicate globally, he was the first to think globally about communication.
paragraphs.zh: 在比尔·盖茨和史蒂夫·乔布斯等标志性人物渗透我们生活的一百年前，一位爱尔兰裔意大利发明家为21世纪的通信大爆炸奠定了基础。古列尔莫·马可尼堪称现代通信领域第一位真正意义上的全球人物。他不仅是第一个实现全球通信的人，更是第一个以全球视角思考通信的人。

paragraphs.en: Marconi may not have been the greatest inventor of his time, but more than anyone else, he brought about a fundamental shift in the way we communicate. Today's globally networked media and communication system has its origins in the 19th century, when, for the first time, messages were sent electronically across great distances.
paragraphs.zh: 马可尼或许并非他那个时代最伟大的发明家，但他比任何人都更深刻地改变了我们的沟通方式。当今全球联网的媒体与通信系统起源于19世纪，那时信息首次以电子方式跨越遥远距离传输。

paragraphs.en: The telegraph, the telephone, and radio were the obvious predecessors of the Internet, iPods, and mobile phones. What made the link from then to now was the development of wireless communication. Marconi was the first to develop and perfect this system, using the recently-discovered "air waves" that make up the electromagnetic spectrum.
paragraphs.zh: 电报、电话和无线电显然是互联网、iPod和移动电话的前身。连接当时与现在的关键，是无线通信的发展。马可尼是第一个开发并完善这一系统的人，他利用了新近发现的构成电磁频谱的“空中电波”。

paragraphs.en: Between 1896, when he applied for his first patent in England at the age of 22, and his death in Italy in 1937, Marconi was at the center of every major innovation in electronic communication. He was also a skilled and sophisticated organizer, an entrepreneurial innovator, who mastered the use of corporate strategy, media relations, government lobbying, international diplomacy, patents, and prosecution.
paragraphs.zh: 从1896年22岁时在英国申请第一项专利，到1937年在意大利去世，马可尼始终处于电子通信每一项重大创新的中心。他还是一位娴熟老练的组织者、富有创业精神的革新者，精通企业战略、媒体关系、政府游说、国际外交、专利及诉讼的运用。

paragraphs.en: Marconi was really interested in only one thing: the extension of mobile, personal, long-distance communication to the ends of the earth. Some like to refer to him as a genius, but if there was any genius to Marconi it was this vision.
paragraphs.zh: 马可尼真正感兴趣的只有一件事：将移动、个人、长距离通信扩展到地球的尽头。有人喜欢称他为天才，但如果说马可尼有什么天才之处，那正是这一愿景。

paragraphs.en: In 1901 he succeeded in signaling across the Atlantic, from the west coast of England to Newfoundland in the USA, despite the claims of science that it could not be done. In 1924 he convinced the British government to encircle the world with a chain of wireless stations using the latest technology that he had devised, shortwave radio.
paragraphs.zh: 1901年，他成功实现了横跨大西洋的信号传输——从英格兰西海岸到美国纽芬兰——尽管当时科学界声称这不可能做到。1924年，他说服英国政府用他发明的最新技术——短波无线电——建立一系列无线电台，覆盖全球。

paragraphs.en: There are some who say Marconi lost his edge when commercial broadcasting came along; he didn't see that radio could or should be used to frivolous ends. In one of his last public speeches, a radio broadcast to the United States in March 1937, he deplored that broadcasting had become a one-way means of communication and foresaw it moving in another direction, toward communication as a means of exchange.
paragraphs.zh: 有人说，商业广播兴起后马可尼失去了优势；他没有看到无线电可以或应该被用于娱乐目的。在1937年3月最后一次对美广播的公开演讲中，他对广播沦为单向传播工具表示遗憾，并预见它将转向另一个方向——成为交流互动的手段。

paragraphs.en: That was visionary genius. Marconi's career was devoted to making wireless communication happen cheaply, efficiently, smoothly, and with an elegance that would appear to be intuitive and uncomplicated to the user. There is a direct connection from Marconi to today's social media, search engines, and program streaming.
paragraphs.zh: 那是具有远见的天才。马可尼的职业生涯致力于让无线通信变得廉价、高效、流畅，并以一种对用户而言直观简单、近乎优雅的方式实现。从马可尼到当今的社交媒体、搜索引擎和节目流媒体，存在着一条直接的联系脉络。

paragraphs.en: In a sense, Marconi's vision jumped from his time to our own. Marconi invented the idea of global communication—or, more straightforwardly, globally networked, mobile, wireless communication. Initially, this was wireless Morse code telegraphy, the principal communication technology of his day.
paragraphs.zh: 从某种意义上说，马可尼的愿景从他的时代直接跨越到了我们的时代。马可尼发明了全球通信的理念——或者更直白地说，是全球联网的、移动的、无线通信。最初，这是无线莫尔斯电码电报，即他那个时代的主要通信技术。

paragraphs.en: Marconi was the first to develop a practical method for wireless telegraphy using radio waves. He borrowed technical details from many sources, but what set him apart was a self-confident vision of the power of communication technology, and of the steps needed to consolidate his own position as a player in that field.
paragraphs.zh: 马可尼是第一个开发出使用无线电波进行无线电报实用方法的人。他借鉴了许多来源的技术细节，但让他与众不同之处在于，他对通信技术力量的自信愿景，以及为巩固自己在该领域地位所需采取步骤的清醒认识。

paragraphs.en: Tracing Marconi's lifeline leads us into the story of modern communication itself. There were other important figures, but Marconi towered over them all in reach, power, and influence, as well as in the grip he had on the popular imagination of his time.
paragraphs.zh: 追溯马可尼的生命轨迹，也就走进了现代通信本身的故事。当时也有其他重要人物，但马可尼在覆盖范围、权力和影响力方面，以及在他对时代大众想象力的掌控上，都远超他们。

paragraphs.en: In his lifetime, Marconi foresaw the development of television and the fax machine, GPS, radar, and the portable hand-held telephone. Two months before he died, newspapers were reporting that he was working on a "death ray," and that he had "killed a rat with an intricate device at a distance of three feet."
paragraphs.zh: 马可尼生前预见到了电视、传真机、GPS、雷达以及便携式手持电话的发展。在他去世前两个月，报纸还在报道他正在研制一种“死亡射线”，并“用一台复杂设备在三英尺外杀死了一只老鼠”。

paragraphs.en: By then, anything Marconi said or did was newsworthy. Stock prices rose or sank according to his pronouncements. If Marconi said he thought it might rain, there was likely to be a run on umbrellas.
paragraphs.zh: 那时，马可尼的任何言行都具有新闻价值。股价随他的言论涨跌。如果马可尼说他认为可能会下雨，市场上很可能出现抢购雨伞的风潮。

paragraphs.en: Marconi's biography is also a story about choices and the motivations behind them. At one level, Marconi could be fiercely autonomous and independent of the constraints of his own social class. On another scale, he was a perpetual outsider.
paragraphs.zh: 马可尼的传记也是一个关于选择及其背后动机的故事。一方面，马可尼可以极度自主，不受自身社会阶层约束。另一方面，他又永远是个局外人。

paragraphs.en: Wherever he went, he was never "of" the group; he was always the "other," considered foreign in Britain, British in Italy, and "not American" in the United States. At the same time, he also suffered tremendously from a need for acceptance that drove, and sometimes stained, every one of his relationships.
paragraphs.zh: 无论走到哪里，他从不真正“属于”那个群体；他总是“异类”，在英国被视为外国人，在意大利被视为英国人，在美国则被视为“非美国人”。与此同时，他深受被接纳需求的煎熬，这种需求推动着他，有时也玷污了他的每一段人际关系。

paragraphs.en: Marconi placed a permanent stamp on the way we live. He was the first person to imagine a practical application for the wireless spectrum, and to develop it successfully into a global communication system—both worldwide and all-inclusive. He was able to do this because of a combination of factors—most important, timing and opportunity.
paragraphs.zh: 马可尼在我们的生活方式上留下了永恒的印记。他是第一个设想无线频谱实际应用的人，并将其成功发展为全球通信系统——既是全球范围的，也是无所不包的。他之所以能做到这一点，是多种因素共同作用的结果——最重要的是时机与机遇。

paragraphs.en: But the single-mindedness and determination with which he carried out his self-imposed mission was fundamentally character-based. Millions of Marconi's contemporaries had the same class, gender, race, and colonial privilege as he, but only a handful did anything with it.
paragraphs.zh: 但他执行自己赋予使命时的一心一意和坚定决心，从根本上源自性格。数以百万计与马可尼同时代的人拥有与他相同的阶级、性别、种族和殖民特权，但只有极少数人有所作为。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, Marconi was uncompromisingly independent intellectually. Shortly after Marconi's death, the nuclear physicist Enrico Fermi wrote that Marconi proved that theory and experimentation were complementary features of progress. Marconi had the advantage of not being burdened by preconceived assumptions.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，马可尼在思想上有着毫不妥协的独立性。马可尼去世后不久，核物理学家恩里科·费米写道，马可尼证明了理论与实验是进步过程中相辅相成的两个方面。马可尼的优势在于，他没有被先入为主的假设所拖累。

paragraphs.en: The most controversial aspect of Marconi's life was his uncritical embrace of Benito Mussolini. At first this was not problematic for him. But as the regressive nature of Mussolini's regime became clear, he began to suffer a crisis of conscience.
paragraphs.zh: 马可尼一生中最具争议之处，在于他对贝尼托·墨索里尼不加批判的拥护。起初这对他的处境并无影响。但随着墨索里尼政权的倒退本质日益显露，他开始陷入良心危机。

paragraphs.en: However, after a lifetime of moving within the circles of power, he was unable to break with authority, and served Mussolini faithfully until the day he died in 1937. He passed away shortly before he would have had to take a stand in the conflict that consumed a world that he had, in part, created.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，在权力圈中摸爬滚打了一生之后，他无法与权威决裂，忠实地为墨索里尼服务，直至1937年去世。他恰好在他不得不对那场冲突表态之前离世——那场冲突吞噬了他亲手参与创造的世界。
